task_spec:
# Belayer Marketing Site

## Problem

Belayer-cli needs a public-facing marketing website to explain what it is and how its pieces fit together to developers already in the Claude Code ecosystem.

## What to Build

A two-page React SPA hosted on Vercel. Rock-climbing themed with an abstract/modern aesthetic (climbing as metaphor, not literal). Uses Framer Motion for subtle scroll-driven animations.

**Source repo:** https://github.com/donovan-yohan/belayer (create a new `site/` directory or a separate repo — implementer's discretion based on repo structure)

## Tech Stack

- Vite + React + TypeScript
- React Router (client-side, two routes)
- Framer Motion (useInView, useScroll, useTransform)
- Tailwind CSS
- Vercel static deployment

## Visual Design

### Palette — "Warm Granite"

- Background base: `#1c1917`, elevated: `#211f1d`
- Warm gradient: `linear-gradient(160deg, #1c1917, #231d18)`
- Deep gradient: `linear-gradient(160deg, #171412, #1c1917)`
- Summit gradient: `linear-gradient(180deg, #1c1917, #231d18, #2a2118)`
- Text primary: `#fafaf9`, secondary: `#a8a29e`, muted: `#57534e`
- Accent (amber): `#f59e0b`
- Border/surface: `#292524`

### Typography

- System font stack, 800 weight for headings
- Hero h1: 72px, line-height 1.05
- Section h2: 48–56px, line-height 1.1 (climax sections: 64px)
- Body: 17–18px, line-height 1.7–1.8
- Labels: 12px uppercase, 4px letter-spacing, amber
- Skill tags: monospace, 14px, surface bg with amber text

### Layout

- Full-viewport sections (100vh min-height)
- Content blocks max 600–700px wide
- Generous padding (80–100px horizontal)
- Left/right alternation, centered for climax moments

## Persistent Elements (Both Pages)

1. **Nav** — Fixed top, blur backdrop. Logo "BELAYER" in amber left, links right (Home, Philosophy, GitHub ↗). Mobile hamburger.
2. **Vertical Progress Bar** — Fixed right edge, 3px track, fills top-to-bottom in amber. Glowing 10px dot tracks position.
3. **Rope Thread** — Fixed left side (~48px), 2px amber at ~25% opacity, gradient fades top/bottom.
4. **Peek Hints** — Bottom of each section teases next section title with bobbing arrow.
5. **Background Numbers** — Giant faded numbers (01–07), ~180–200px, 900 weight, ~3% white opacity, parallax at 0.3x scroll speed.

## Page 1: Home

Full-page sections, big bold typography, alternating left/right alignment. Brief content — headline + 2-3 sentences per section.

1. **Hero** (centered) — "Your **safety system** for AI development" + CTAs
2. **Harness** (left, bg-elevated) — "The Core"
3. **Plan** (right, bg-warm) — "Chart the **route**"
4. **Implement** (left, bg-deep) — "Make the **climb**"
5. **Reflect** (right, bg-elevated) — "Check your **anchors**"
6. **Spotter** (left, bg-warm) — "Code Review"
7. **Setter** (right, bg-deep) — "Orchestration"
8. **Belayer** (centered, bg-base + radial amber glow, 64px heading) — CTAs

### Animations
- Fade-in + slight upward drift (translateY 20px → 0), 0.4s ease-out
- Staggered: label → headline → body (~0.1s intervals)
- Triggered at ~30% viewport intersection
- Background numbers parallax via useScroll + useTransform

## Page 2: Philosophy ("The Meta Framework")

Split layout: headline on one side, detailed content on the other. Alternates left/right. Presentation-style — more content per section than home.

1. **Hero** (centered) — "Built from the **bottom up**"
2. **Opening a PR** (headline left, content right, bg-elevated) — The first pain point, `pr:author`
3. **Reviewing Code** (headline right, content left, bg-warm) — `pr:review` as spotter
4. **The Harness** (headline left, content right, bg-deep) — `harness:plan`, `harness:brainstorm`, `harness:reflect`
5. **The Setter** (headline right, content left, bg-elevated) — Orchestration layer
6. **Belayer is the Glue** (centered, bg-summit + radial glow) — Skill tags displayed
7. **The Principle** (headline left, content right, bg-base) — "Solve the smallest problem first" + CTAs

### Animations
- Same fade-in + drift as home
- Split: headline appears first, content fades in ~0.2s after
- Skill tags stagger in at 0.05s each

## Component Architecture

```
src/
  components/
    Layout.tsx          — Nav + progress bar + rope thread wrapper
    Nav.tsx             — Fixed navigation
    ProgressBar.tsx     — Vertical scroll progress (right edge)
    RopeThread.tsx      — Fixed vertical line (left edge)
    Section.tsx         — Full-page section wrapper (bg variants, alignment)
    SplitSection.tsx    — Split layout variant (headline + content sides)
    PeekHint.tsx        — "Next section" teaser at bottom
    SkillTag.tsx        — Monospace skill/command tag
    Button.tsx          — Primary/secondary CTA buttons
  pages/
    Home.tsx
    Philosophy.tsx
  hooks/
    useScrollProgress.ts
  App.tsx
  main.tsx
  index.css             — Tailwind directives + custom properties
```

## Responsive Behavior

- **Desktop (>1024px):** Full split layouts, large typography, side margins
- **Tablet (768–1024px):** Reduced font sizes, narrower margins, split layouts stack
- **Mobile (<768px):** Single column, full-height sections, nav → hamburger, progress bar hidden/thinned, rope thread hidden

## Deployment

- Vercel static deployment
- `vite build` → `dist/`
- SPA fallback via `vercel.json`: `{ "rewrites": [{ "source": "/(.*)", "destination": "/index.html" }] }`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Site loads with warm granite palette and climbing-metaphor aesthetic
- Home page scrolls through all 8 sections with fade-in animations and parallax
- Philosophy page tells the bottom-up narrative across 7 presentation-style slides
- Vertical progress bar tracks scroll position on both pages
- Navigation switches between pages without full reload
- Responsive down to mobile
- Deployed and accessible on Vercel

## Design Reference

Full design spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-09-belayer-marketing-site-design.md`
Visual mockups: `.superpowers/brainstorm/65132-1773111560/` (home-design-v2.html, philosophy-design.html)
description: Scaffold the Vite + React + TypeScript project with Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion, and React Router. Set up the file structure, Tailwind config with warm granite palette tokens, vercel.json for SPA fallback, and a basic App.tsx with two routes rendering placeholder pages.
